frontmatter.description: 403 forbidden — user authenticated but lacks required role/permission

signature.embedding_seed: rbac forbidden permission role denied authorization

body:
## When this fires
403 responses indicate the user is authenticated but RBAC denied the action.

## Playbook
1. Inspect rbac-policy-current to confirm the policy expected for this resource.
2. Read user-roles-snapshot to verify the user's effective roles match what they need.
3. If the policy is correct and the user lacks roles, escalate to the owning team.

## Common confusion
This is *not* [[auth-401-jwt-refresh]]: 401 means the token itself failed validation, 403 means the token was valid but the principal isn't allowed.
